description: The `/coding` behavior convergence work is tracked as a new harness task so future sessions can resume from the completed-harness, `/coding status`, and notification-policy decisions instead of rediscovering them live.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only tracks the `/coding` behavior convergence harness.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry records the three target changes: completed-harness direct launch, `/coding status`, and push-notification suppression.
Confirm the scope remains limited to nanobot orchestration and Telegram behavior, without changing the target repo harness format or Codex worker execution model.

description: Completed repo harnesses no longer block new Telegram coding tasks; nanobot treats them as historical context and launches the new task immediately.
steps: Create a repo with PLAN.json, PROGRESS.md, and init.sh whose PLAN.json is fully complete, then send a Telegram-style `/coding ...` request.
Verify the task goes directly to `starting` instead of `waiting_user` and that no `repo_completed_harness` conflict metadata is written.
Confirm an active unfinished harness still follows the explicit confirmation path before launch.

description: Telegram supports `/coding status` as an explicit coding-task status command while preserving the existing Chinese `状态` control path.
steps: Send `/coding status` for a private-chat coding task and verify it returns the same detailed status content as `状态`.
Verify `/coding status` does not create a new coding task or get parsed as repo `status`.
Verify `/coding ...` with a real repo name still creates and launches a task as before.

description: Telegram proactive coding-task notifications are limited to start, completion, failure, and required user-confirmation events; running progress no longer pushes continuously.
steps: Trigger a starting task and verify one start notification is emitted to Telegram.
Advance a task to `running` and verify notifier sends no new progress push from poll-loop updates alone.
Verify `waiting_user`, `completed`, and `failed` continue to emit their dedicated notifications.

description: Detailed coding-task diagnostics remain available through explicit status views even after proactive running notifications are disabled.
steps: Run `nanobot coding-task status <id>` and verify the CLI still shows repo, goal, recent progress, live report, and tmux metadata.
Send Telegram `状态` and `/coding status` for the same task and verify both return the detailed diagnostic view.
Confirm the notification policy change only affects background push behavior, not explicit status inspection.

description: Focused tests cover completed-harness routing, `/coding status`, and the reduced notification policy without regressing existing active-harness controls.
steps: Run focused pytest for coding-task routing, notifier behavior, and CLI status coverage.
Verify tests cover the completed-harness direct-launch path, `/coding status` lookup, and suppression of running-task pushes.
Confirm `python -m compileall` still succeeds for the touched coding-task modules and tests.

description: README and PROGRESS.md document the new `/coding` defaults, including completed-harness direct launch, `/coding status`, and the reduced Telegram push policy.
steps: Open README.md and verify it documents `/coding status`, the direct-launch behavior for completed harnesses, and the new push-notification policy.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify it records the baseline, implementation decisions, and verification evidence for this harness task.
Confirm PLAN.json is fully marked complete after the implementation and verification pass.
